verdict.summary: Guide is broadly useful on the current 5-year naturalisation route, but it contains major unsupported/post-April-2026 claims about the nationality reform, internal contradictions on the criminal threshold, and several misleading practical details.

issues.current: Decreto AR 48/XVII ... промульгирован Президентом Сегуру 03.05.2026, ждёт публикации в Diário da República
issues.problem: As of the April 2026 reference point, the nationality reform approved on 01.04.2026 was awaiting promulgation. The guide presents later May 2026 events as settled facts, which are outside the stated verification basis and are not acceptable without the actual Diário da República / Presidency record.
issues.suggestion: Replace with: "Статус на апрель 2026: реформа Lei da Nacionalidade была одобрена Assembleia da República 01.04.2026 и ожидала промульгации/вето Президента и последующей публикации в Diário da República. До вступления в силу действует прежняя редакция Lei 37/81."
issues.source: Assembleia da República / Lei 37/81; developer reference: new nationality law approved 01.04.2026 awaiting promulgation

issues.current: Промульгирован Президентом Сегуру 03.05.2026... На 13.05.2026 публикация в Diário da República ВСЁ ЕЩЁ НЕ состоялась
issues.problem: This is a post-April chronology and contradicts the required baseline that, as of April 2026, the law was only approved and awaiting promulgation. It should not be stated as law-status fact in an April-2026 review unless supported by official post-April sources and the guide scope is explicitly updated.
issues.suggestion: State only that Decreto AR 48/XVII / the nationality reform was approved by Parliament on 01.04.2026 and was pending presidential promulgation/publication; clearly mark all effects as proposed/not yet in force.
issues.source: Assembleia da República; Diário da República publication status

issues.current: Новый закон (Decreto AR 48/XVII) — промульгирован 03.05.2026
issues.problem: Incorrect for an April-2026 status guide. The reform was approved on 01.04.2026 but not yet promulgated/published/in force at the stated reference point.
issues.suggestion: Rename to: "Реформа Lei da Nacionalidade, одобренная 01.04.2026 — ожидает промульгации и публикации".
issues.source: Assembleia da República; Diário da República

issues.current: 03.05.2026 — Президент Сегуру промульгировал Decreto AR 48/XVII... 08.05.2026... 12.05.2026...
issues.problem: The guide relies on events after April 2026 and treats them as established legal status. This is inconsistent with the April 2026 review basis and should not be mixed into a 2026 naturalisation guide unless the entire guide is re-baselined to mid-May 2026 with official sources.
issues.suggestion: Remove the May chronology or put it in a clearly dated update box with official Presidency/TC/DRE links. For April 2026, say: "После одобрения 01.04.2026 текст ожидал решения Президента; до публикации в DRE юридической силы изменения не имеют."
issues.source: Diário da República; Presidência da República; Tribunal Constitucional

issues.current: Отсутствие судимостей с наказанием ≥ 3 лет лишения свободы — в любой стране проживания
issues.problem: The substance is broadly aligned with current Article 6 wording, but it conflicts with multiple later statements saying the new reform lowers the threshold from 5 to 3 years. Both cannot be true.
issues.suggestion: Keep the current-law threshold as "pena de prisão igual ou superior a 3 anos" and remove all claims that the reform changes it "from 5 to 3" unless the approved reform text actually changes a different category. If discussing the reform, explain precisely what changes compared with the current Lei 37/81.

issues.current: После вступления 48/XVII в силу: ... порог судимости снижен с 5 до 3 лет лишения свободы
issues.problem: Internal contradiction: the guide itself correctly states under current law that the disqualifying criminal threshold is already ≥3 years. Saying it will be lowered from 5 to 3 is misleading.
issues.suggestion: Delete "порог судимости снижен с 5 до 3 лет" or rewrite with the exact legal change, if any, based on the final approved text.

issues.current: порог судимости снижен с 5 до 3 лет лишения свободы
issues.problem: Contradicts the guide's own current-law section and the current Article 6 naturalisation threshold.
issues.suggestion: Remove or correct this sentence. Current naturalisation already uses the 3-year threshold.

issues.current: Порог криминальной чистоты ужесточён: с 5 до 3 лет лишения свободы
issues.problem: Factually misleading for the standard naturalisation requirement, because the current rule already refers to conviction with prison sentence equal to or above 3 years.
issues.suggestion: Replace with a precise description from the reform text or remove this bullet.

issues.current: Заявления, поданные ДО вступления нового закона в силу — сам ТЕКСТ закона содержит переходное положение...
issues.problem: The existence and exact wording/effect of the transitional provision are presented as final, but as of April 2026 the reform was not promulgated or published. Transitional rules can only be relied on after the final text is published in Diário da República.
issues.suggestion: Write: "Проект/одобренный Парламентом текст предусматривал переходные положения, но их окончательное действие нужно проверять по опубликованной версии в Diário da República."

issues.current: По разъяснению IRN от 07–08.05.2026, ключевая отсечка — это дата подачи на онлайн-платформе
issues.problem: This relies on a May 2026 alleged/press-reported clarification and is not available under the April 2026 status. Also, for a sensitive cut-off rule, a news article alone is not enough; the guide should cite an official IRN notice or the final law.
issues.suggestion: For April 2026, say: "Если закон изменится, критическую дату и переходные правила нужно проверять по опубликованному закону и официальным инструкциям IRN." Add an official IRN source if retaining the online-submission rule.
issues.source: IRN official notice needed; Diário da República

issues.current: PÚBLICO — IRN: pedidos de nacionalidade contam desde data de submissão. Pendentes não são afectados (08.05.2026)
issues.problem: A press article is insufficient as the primary source for a legally critical transitional rule. The guide needs the official IRN communication or the final published law.
issues.suggestion: Add/cite official IRN guidance, the Portal da Nacionalidade notice, or the final Diário da República law text. Keep news only as secondary context.

issues.problem: The guide claims verification and updates after the April 2026 baseline. This makes the status ambiguous: either it is an April 2026 guide, in which case May events must be removed, or it is a May 2026 guide, in which case the official post-April legal sources must be complete and final.
issues.suggestion: Align all status fields to one date. If using April 2026, set the reform status to "approved 01.04.2026, awaiting promulgation". If using May 2026, cite official Presidency/TC/IRN/DRE sources and verify whether publication occurred.

issues.problem: Contradicts the required April 2026 review point and increases the risk that the guide includes unverified future/post-cutoff developments.
issues.suggestion: Either change the verification basis to May 2026 and support it with official sources, or remove May developments and set verification to an April 2026 date.

issues.current: Certidão de residência legal ... подтверждает даты всех ВНЖ и периоды ожидания
issues.problem: Misleading. AIMA certificates generally evidence legal residence/permit periods; whether they include or prove pre-permit waiting time after a residence request is a separate and sensitive issue after the 2024 nationality amendment. Users should not assume the AIMA certificate will automatically certify all waiting periods for nationality counting.
issues.suggestion: Rewrite: "Certidão de residência legal от AIMA подтверждает зарегистрированные периоды легального проживания/ВНЖ. Для зачёта периода между подачей запроса на первый ВНЖ и выдачей карты могут потребоваться дополнительные доказательства и позиция IRN/AIMA; проверяйте по конкретному делу."
issues.source: AIMA; Lei 37/81 as amended by Lei Orgânica 1/2024

issues.current: Certidão de residência legal от AIMA — подтверждает точные даты ВНЖ и периодов ожидания
issues.problem: Overstates what the certificate proves. The certificate is useful, but it should not be described as definitively confirming all waiting periods for the 2024 residence-request counting rule.
issues.suggestion: Say it confirms AIMA's recorded residence data and may help support the 5-year calculation; for waiting-time credit, include proof of the residence request and subsequent grant and expect IRN verification.

issues.current: Certidão de residência legal AIMA (опционально) — 15 €
issues.problem: AIMA fee tables changed on 01.03.2026. The fixed €15 value is likely outdated and should not be hardcoded without checking the current AIMA emoluments table.
issues.suggestion: Replace with: "сверяйте по актуальной tabela de taxas/emolumentos AIMA, действующей с 01.03.2026" and update the amount from the current official table.

issues.problem: Contradicts the timeline text saying actual processing in 2026 is 24–48 months. 720 days is under 24 months and nowhere near 48 months.
issues.suggestion: Set timelineDaysMin/timelineDaysMax consistently with the text, e.g. approximately 730–1460 days, or change the text to match 365–720 days.

issues.current: Фактический срок в 2026 году — 24–48 месяцев
issues.problem: This conflicts with top-level timelineDaysMin/timelineDaysMax of 365–720 days. The guide gives two different processing ranges.
issues.suggestion: Use one consistent estimate across the guide. If 24–48 months is intended, top-level range should be about 730–1460 days.

issues.problem: The CPLP variant gives a much shorter processing time than the general 24–48 month estimate without explanation. If this refers to CPLP residence processes rather than nationality, it is misplaced; if it refers to nationality, it needs a source.
issues.suggestion: Either remove the CPLP-specific timeline or explain with an official/statistical source why CPLP naturalisation is expected to take only 6–18 months.

issues.current: Длительный выезд > 6 месяцев подряд или > 8 месяцев в сумме за 2 года может прервать срок — зависит от типа ВНЖ
issues.problem: This mixes residence-permit validity/cancellation rules with nationality residence counting. Absence limits depend on the permit regime and renewal rules; they do not automatically reset nationality eligibility in every case.
issues.suggestion: Rewrite as: "Длительные отсутствия могут создать проблемы с сохранением/продлением конкретного типа ВНЖ и с доказательством реального проживания; сами лимиты зависят от статуса. Для гражданства IRN проверяет наличие законного проживания за требуемый период."

issues.current: Онлайн через Portal da Nacionalidade (irn.justica.gov.pt)
issues.problem: The URL is imprecise/misleading. The official service page is on justica.gov.pt, while the Portal da Nacionalidade has its own portal address. Users may not land on the actual submission portal.
issues.suggestion: Use the official service link already in sources: https://justica.gov.pt/Servicos/Submeter-pedido-de-nacionalidade and, if mentioning the portal, add the current Portal da Nacionalidade URL as shown by IRN/Justiça.

issues.current: Свидетельство о рождении — апостиль + присяжный перевод на португальский
issues.problem: The phrase "присяжный перевод" is misleading for Portugal, and although a later warning clarifies this, the checklist still uses the wrong shorthand.
issues.suggestion: Change to: "перевод на португальский, надлежаще заверенный способом, принимаемым IRN; апостиль/консульская легализация оригинала при необходимости".

issues.current: Справка о несудимости страны гражданства + каждой страны проживания ≥ 1 года за последние 5 лет
issues.problem: This is a common practical rule, but the exact requirement can vary by nationality route and IRN request; it should cite IRN/Regulamento wording. For a sensitive document requirement, a government source should be explicit.
issues.suggestion: Add a source/caveat: "по требованиям IRN/Regulamento da Nacionalidade; IRN может запросить дополнительные certificates depending on residence history".

issues.current: Для граждан CPLP ... сертификат НЕ нужен: португальский считается родным/официальным
issues.problem: Too categorical. The waiver is tied to being from a country where Portuguese is an official language / proving knowledge under the regulation, not merely to a political label in a user-friendly way. Edge cases such as documentation, place of birth, and IRN interpretation should be caveated.
issues.suggestion: Write: "Для заявителей из стран, где португальский является официальным языком, IRN обычно не требует CIPLE, но проверяйте применимость по вашей ситуации и документам."

issues.current: Presidência da República — Promulgação Decreto AR 48/XVII ... lastRetrieved 2026-05-04
issues.problem: This source is post-April and cannot support an April-2026 status statement. If retained, the guide is no longer an April-2026 guide and needs complete May verification including DRE publication status.
issues.suggestion: For April 2026, remove this source and cite Parliament approval. For May 2026, verify against official Presidency and Diário da República pages.

issues.current: Observador — Seguro veta por inconstitucionalidade ... 12.05.2026
issues.problem: News source postdating the April 2026 baseline. Also, constitutional-law status should preferably cite Tribunal Constitucional and Presidency directly.
issues.suggestion: If discussing this at all, add official Tribunal Constitucional acórdão and Presidency veto/devolution notice; otherwise remove for an April-2026 guide.

missing_topics: Official Diário da República source for the final published text/status of the 2026 nationality reform, if the guide is kept as a May 2026 guide
Official IRN source for the alleged 07–08.05.2026 online-submission cut-off rule; news sources alone are insufficient
Clear statement that until publication and entry into force, the 5-year regime under the existing Lei 37/81 continues to apply
More precise explanation of the 2024 rule counting time from the residence-permit request, including what evidence is needed and whether AIMA certifies that period
Updated AIMA fee reference reflecting the fee table effective 01.03.2026
Clear separation between residence-permit absence/cancellation rules and nationality eligibility counting
